what_it_does: Defines a self-contained HTML view that styles a centered almanac-like slab, fetches story content for id "workshop" and node "B3", then renders the node's super text, title, figure, figure label, and bullets into the page.
observations: Uses Google-hosted Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono plus /surface/styles/piece.css.
Inline CSS builds a centered warm-toned slab with typographic hierarchy, large figure text, bullet rows, and a provenance footer.
The page loads /surface/runtime/surface.js and relies on a global DNA object for escaping and story-node lookup.
The binding is hard-coded as { id: "workshop", node: "B3", tier: "leaf" }.
Content is fetched from /api/content/workshop with cache set to no-store.
Render failure states are written into the title area for missing nodes or unavailable content.
The provenance text states that words are resolved from content/stories/workshop.json, node B3, at render time.
standouts: Strong separation between presentation addresses via CSS variables and words resolved from fetched content.
The file makes the content binding explicit in both code and comments.
The UI includes an on-page provenance footer that exposes the content source and node identity.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, radius, shadow, and type scale.
Uses semantic-lite class names for presentation blocks and ids for script targets.
Uses an async IIFE for fetch, lookup, and DOM population.
Escapes string content before inserting title and bullet text as HTML.
Keeps data selection file-local through a single BIND constant.
concerns: Depends on global DNA APIs without local guards beyond the outer catch block.
Uses innerHTML for title and bullet rendering, though text values are passed through DNA.esc.
Error handling collapses different fetch, JSON, runtime, and missing dependency failures into one content-unavailable message.
The hard-coded provenance text can drift from the BIND constant if edited independently.
notes: A render-time story-node view: fixed presentation shell, hard-coded workshop/B3 binding, fetched content, and visible provenance.
questions: What exact shape is guaranteed for the fetched story tree and node fields?
Should the displayed provenance be generated from BIND instead of duplicated literal text?
Is tier "leaf" consumed elsewhere or only documentary in this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file presents itself as an output of a content language and renders words from a resolved story node rather than embedding the visible copy directly.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate a piece whose visual form is local but whose displayed language is resolved from a story tree through an explicit node binding.
novelty: The unusual part is the visible provenance contract inside a polished visual slab, making the content source and node part of the rendered artifact.
contribution: Contributes a node-bound HTML presentation pattern that a fused system would keep as an example of token-addressed styling, runtime story resolution, explicit binding metadata, and visible provenance.
summary_for_embedding: This is an HTML piece that renders a styled almanac slab by fetching the workshop story, resolving node B3 through DNA.story.node, and injecting its fields into predefined DOM regions.
intention_for_embedding: It was for demonstrating a design artifact whose words come from a content tree at render time while the file supplies the visual substrate and binding.
